title: Uplifted Fluffy Argentinian Lentils Stew
description: A hearty, grassy stew with a mustard bhuna base, cooked to fluffy perfection in a tandoor clay pot. It is diabetic-friendly and perfect for a comforting dinner.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the tandoor clay pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, stirring frequently.
Add the garlic, cumin, coriander, turmeric, mustard seeds, and mustard powder, and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the lentils and vegetable broth, bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low and cover.
Simmer for 45 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the lentils are tender and the mixture has thickened to a fluffy consistency.
Remove the pot from the heat, fluff the lentils with a fork, and let it rest for 5 minutes.
Stir in the fresh cilantro and adjust seasoning with salt if desired.
Serve hot, spooning the stew into bowls directly from the tandoor clay pot, and enjoy.
